stories.headline: Jaxson Dart's fixin' to gush — and the saloon still ain't payin' full price
stories.read: The analysts keep naming the Giants' young gun a breakout for 2026, and pardner, the numbers back the swagger: 24 scores and near 500 on the ground as a greenhorn rookie. A signal-caller who runs for paydirt is a vein that pays double in this game. Grab him a few rounds past where the herd's takin' the safe horses and you've struck cheap gold at the one spot everybody overpays for.

stories.headline: Olave comes off the blood thinners June 29 — the laid-up star's about to ride again
stories.read: Word off the wire is the Saints receiver sheds his thinners June 29 and points toward full-go come training camp after that nasty blood clot scare laid him up. Spooked managers are lettin' him slide down the board, and that's exactly the dip a sharp prospector waits for. Buy the discount on a true No. 1 before he's cleared and the price jumps back up the canyon.

stories.headline: Etienne rode off — Jacksonville's backfield is wide-open range now
stories.read: Travis Etienne packed his wagon, and coach Coen says Tank Bigsby's full-go for camp while Bhayshul Tuten's reportedly been sharp on the OTA range. That's unclaimed territory, pardner — somebody's gonna haul the touches and nobody's paid for it yet. Stake your claim on Tuten as the cheap dart and keep the spare horse saddled; first one to strike this vein wins the showdown.
